title: Feeling GAN training through the forger-and-detective game
tagline: Walk through seven steps of the adversarial game — manual discriminator, optimal D*, generator gradients, mode collapse, WGAN fix
summary: A pedagogical explainer on how GAN training actually works. You'll pick a target distribution, tune a manual discriminator with a slider, derive the optimal D*(x) by hand, click to see the generator's gradient arrow on a single sample, scrub training steps to watch the Nash equilibrium emerge, witness mode collapse on bimodal data, and close with why Wasserstein distance stabilised training in 2017.
highlights: Step 1 · pick a target: bimodal / skewed / three-mode distribution.
Step 2 · build a manual discriminator with centre and slope sliders; watch BCE loss respond.
Step 3 · slide generator σ and see the optimal D*(x) = p/(p+p_G) reshape itself.
Step 4 · click anywhere to drop a generator sample and see G's gradient arrow.
Step 5 · scrub training steps; watch G's distribution glide onto real and D(x) flatten to 0.5.
Step 6 · toggle healthy vs collapsed generator to feel mode collapse.
Three misconception cards, plus a bonus Wasserstein/diffusion comparison.
